programme.title: The Vigil
programme.desc: Terror strikes when an Orthodox Jewish man agrees to watch over the dead body of a community member in a dilapidated Brooklyn home.

programme.title: Ride
programme.desc: Struggling actor James pays his bills by driving for a ride-sharing service in Los Angeles. His night soon takes a shocking turn when Bruno, armed with a gun, takes James and a woman on a terrifying, white-knuckle trip that spirals out of control.

programme.title: Queen of Earth
programme.desc: Tensions rise when an emotionally fragile woman (Elisabeth Moss) spends one week with her best friend (Katherine Waterston).

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: Freeway Killer
programme.desc: Californian serial killer William Bonin (Scott Anthony Leet) searches for victims on the state's highways.

programme.title: Apartment 4E

programme.title: The Beta Test
programme.desc: A married Hollywood agent receives a mysterious letter for an anonymous sexual encounter and becomes ensnared in a sinister world of lying, murder and infidelity.

programme.title: Black '47
programme.desc: In 1847 an Irish mercenary fighting for the British army abandons his post to seek revenge on those responsible for the death of his family.

programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: Nitram

programme.title: The Stanford Prison Experiment
programme.desc: In 1971, Stanford's Professor Philip Zimbardo (Billy Crudup) conducts a controversial psychology experiment in which college students pretend to be either prisoners or guards, but the proceedings soon get out of hand.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: The Dry
programme.desc: A federal agent investigates the decades-old death of a teenage girl in his drought-stricken hometown.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

When Earth's air becomes unbreathable, a message from the future sends a man on a dangerous mission to an unknown world to save the human race.

programme.desc: A woman finds a VHS tape that shows a series of gruesome tales.

programme.title: Almost Human
programme.desc: Seth sees his best friend disappear in a flash of light. Two years later, Seth is still dealing with the fallout and suspicions when a series of grisly murders rocks the community.

programme.title: ATM
programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: Hatching

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Nature documentary looking at a post office in Antarctica that is surrounded by 3,000 gentoo penguins.

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: In 1976, David Attenborough embarked on his most ambitious project ever, Life on Earth. No-one had ever attempted a natural history series on this scale before, and David’s reputation was on the line. This is the remarkable story behind the BBC’s most famous wildlife blockbuster: a three-year, hair-raising odyssey around the world, travelling to 40 countries and filming over 600 species, including the famous mountain gorillas. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenborough’s career as the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of our time.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first ever episode of the landmark natural history series Life on Earth. David Attenborough explores the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.

programme.title: Goolagong
programme.desc: Evonne Goolagong's Wimbledon debut prompts memories of her rise from Barellan to tennis stardom, while she struggles with the controlling behaviour of her coach Vic Edwards.

programme.title: Goolagong
programme.desc: Evonne navigates controversy, pressure and the fight for equal pay, while personal loss and burnout drive her to walk away from tennis at the peak of her career.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend. The townsfolk do not look kindly on yet more intrusion into their still raw lives, but Scarsden begins to doubt the accepted version of events of that tragic day.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners. He discovers that Mandy Bond is the estranged daughter of Harley Reagan, but when he visits Reagan on his out-of-the-way estate, he comes across two sets of skeletal remains.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Count your blessings, they say. For some early retirement can be a blessing - for Hyacinth's Richard, this particular blessing comes very heavily disguised.

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: In 1976, David Attenborough embarked on his most ambitious project ever, Life on Earth. No-one had ever attempted a natural history series on this scale before, and David’s reputation was on the line. This is the remarkable story behind the BBC’s most famous wildlife blockbuster: a three-year, hair-raising odyssey around the world, travelling to 40 countries and filming over 600 species, including the famous mountain gorillas. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenborough’s career as the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of our time.

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Esther Rantzen finds peace and tranquillity at Salisbury Cathedral, Jack Charlton visits Warkworth Castle in Northumberland and Dora Bryan celebrates the beauty of ballrooms.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Timeshift journeys back to a lost era of rail travel, when trains had names, character and style. Once the pride of the railway companies that ran them, the named train is now largely consigned to railway history. Writer and presenter Andrew Martin asks why we once named trains and why we don't do so any more. He embarks on three railway journeys around Britain, following the routes of three of the most famous named trains - the Flying Scotsman, the Cornish Riviera Express and the Brighton Belle. We reflect on travel during the golden age of railways - when the journey itself was as important as reaching your destination - and compare those same journeys with the passenger experience today.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael Palin continues to trace Hemingway's journey. He arrives in Key West in time for Hemingway's 100th birthday celebrations, where he is given boxing lessons from 83-year-old Shine Forbes, who sparred with Hemingway. In Uganda, Michael has his hair cut en route up the Nile. At Butiaba, Uganda he visits the site of a plane crash Hemingway was involved in. Finally, Michael reaches Venice in time for the carnival and then tries his hand at duck-hunting in nearby Caorle.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael Palin stays in Ernest Hemingway's favourite Cuban hotel and samples a few of his favourite hangouts, including Bodeguita del Medio and the Floridita in old Havana. He meets 101-year-old Gregorio Fuentes, considered an Old Man of the Sea, and who sits in the sun, smoking cigars. He peers into Hemingway's Havana house that has now become a shrine where visitors aren't allowed to tread. Back in West America, Michael attempts to be a cowboy and partakes in some male bonding while visiting Yellowstone Park. He also visits the house in Ketchum, Idaho, where Hemingway died in 1961 and where he was laid to rest.

programme.title: Five to Eleven
programme.desc: Emma Thompson reads poetry and prose.

programme.title: Sondheim at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the work of one of the great songwriters and lyricists, Stephen Sondheim, who passed away in November 2021 at the age of 91. A giant of musical theatre, Sondheim first found international fame in 1957 as the lyricist for Leonard Bernstein’s West Side Story and would inspire generations from that moment on. This selection includes some of Sondheim’s finest songs, broadcast on the BBC over a period of 60 years, with performances from a cast of his friends and fans that features some of the biggest names of stage and screen, including Shirley Bassey, Sammy Davis Jr, Michael Ball and Liza Minnelli.

programme.title: Britain's Most Fragile Treasure
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez unlocks the secrets of a centuries-old masterpiece in glass, the Great East Window at York Minster, the largest expanse of medieval stained glass in the UK.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael Palin continues to trace Hemingway's journey. He arrives in Key West in time for Hemingway's 100th birthday celebrations, where he is given boxing lessons from 83-year-old Shine Forbes, who sparred with Hemingway. In Uganda, Michael has his hair cut en route up the Nile. At Butiaba, Uganda he visits the site of a plane crash Hemingway was involved in. Finally, Michael reaches Venice in time for the carnival and then tries his hand at duck-hunting in nearby Caorle.

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael Palin stays in Ernest Hemingway's favourite Cuban hotel and samples a few of his favourite hangouts, including Bodeguita del Medio and the Floridita in old Havana. He meets 101-year-old Gregorio Fuentes, considered an Old Man of the Sea, and who sits in the sun, smoking cigars. He peers into Hemingway's Havana house that has now become a shrine where visitors aren't allowed to tread. Back in West America, Michael attempts to be a cowboy and partakes in some male bonding while visiting Yellowstone Park. He also visits the house in Ketchum, Idaho, where Hemingway died in 1961 and where he was laid to rest.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Timeshift journeys back to a lost era of rail travel, when trains had names, character and style. Once the pride of the railway companies that ran them, the named train is now largely consigned to railway history. Writer and presenter Andrew Martin asks why we once named trains and why we don't do so any more. He embarks on three railway journeys around Britain, following the routes of three of the most famous named trains - the Flying Scotsman, the Cornish Riviera Express and the Brighton Belle. We reflect on travel during the golden age of railways - when the journey itself was as important as reaching your destination - and compare those same journeys with the passenger experience today.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Phase two of the quarter-final stage begins tonight. Two teams who won their first quarter-finals return to play again, looking for the second win that will secure them a place in the semis. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three former flatmates and graduates of Exeter University pit their wits against a news producer, a civil servant and an IT consultant who share a passion for fine food. Who will win the battle as they compete to draw together the connections between things, which, at first glance, seem utterly random?

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Art historian Dr Nina Ramirez reveals the codes and messages hidden in Anglo-Saxon art. From the beautiful jewellery that adorned the first violent pagan invaders through to the stunning Christian manuscripts they would become famous for, she explores the beliefs and ideas that shaped Anglo-Saxon art. Examining many of the greatest Anglo-Saxon treasures - such as the Sutton Hoo artefacts, the Staffordshire hoard, the Franks Casket and the Lindisfarne Gospels - Dr Ramirez charts 600 years of artistic development which was stopped dead in its tracks by the Norman Conquest.

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: How Warhol both glorified and critiqued American culture on his journey from childhood poverty in Pittsburgh to the A-list of New York society.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Mass immigration at the turn of the century confirmed America as a multicultural society but also brought increased poverty and slum life. Robert Hughes examines how this was often reflected in art.

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: In the final episode, Simon Sebag Montefiore follows the Habsburgs to their dramatic demise. From his struggles with Napoleon III and Bismarck, and the suicide of his son Rudolf, to the assassination of his beautiful wife Sisi, Emperor Franz Josef found his empire and family impossible to control. But while the Habsburgs headed for extinction, Vienna blossomed. As the theories of Freud and the sensuality of the secession artists like Klimt and Schiele ushered in the modern age, Hitler and Stalin stalked her streets. It was here that World War I was sparked and World War II was dreamed.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three former flatmates and graduates of Exeter University pit their wits against a news producer, a civil servant and an IT consultant who share a passion for fine food. Who will win the battle as they compete to draw together the connections between things, which, at first glance, seem utterly random?

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons

From the beautiful jewellery that adorned the first violent pagan invaders through to the stunning Christian manuscripts they would become famous for, she explores the beliefs and ideas that shaped Anglo-Saxon art. Examining many of the greatest Anglo-Saxon treasures - such as the Sutton Hoo artefacts, the Staffordshire hoard, the Franks Casket and the Lindisfarne Gospels - Dr Ramirez charts 600 years of artistic development which was stopped dead in its tracks by the Norman Conquest.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Shiona McCallum visits Fifa HQ in Zurich to see some of the latest tech being used in the World Cup. Martin Sharkey explores how changes in the way we watch content are shaping new technologies. Paul Carter hits the racetrack to see if AI technology systems can make journeys safer, and Andrew Rogers meets the dogs being helped to walk and run again thanks to assistive mobility aids.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Across the world, fashion is a fundamental expression of who we are. Today, people in China are rediscovering the beauty of their extraordinarily rich clothing heritage. In this programme, Alastair discovers how historic fashion design still resonates with people in China today, and how it has become a way in which people can celebrate their identity.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: World Questions
programme.desc: A special edition of World Questions, putting young Moroccan women at the centre of a national debate about rights, faith and family law reform. Jonny Dymond chairs a panel of feminist activists and conservatives as they debate issues such as child marriage, sex outside marriage, inheritance, harassment and polygamy. As a new generation pushes for change, Morocco stands out in the Muslim world, with reform being debated openly, and this programme hears from young women at a pivotal moment in civil society.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Following the sentencing of 13 people for violent disorder during protests in Southampton, attention has turned to the role of content creators who film demonstrations and public disorder. The judge cited the usefulness of footage filmed by so-called 'citizen journalists'. Siddharth Venkataramakrishnan of the Institute for Strategic Dialogue and content creator Wesley Winter give their perspective on the issue. The BBC has begun setting out changes as part of plans to deliver significant savings across the organisation. The measures include the closure of several programmes across radio and current affairs output, alongside wider reductions in staffing. Jonathan Munro, global director of BBC News, outlines the changes. Norma Percy has spent years getting to the heart of some of the biggest moments in recent history. Her documentaries have been celebrated for talking directly to the people who made the decisions. As the BBC airs her latest film - about Brexit - she reflects on her storied career and the future of documentary-making.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Train enthusiast Francis Bourgeois travels to Malaysia to explore a railway network dating back 140 years. Jumping on board the iconic Jungle Railway, he experiences one of Southeast Asia's most spectacular train journeys, before arriving in the bustling capital city, Kuala Lumpur, and finishing up on Penang Island for their spectacular Lunar New Year celebrations. En route he joins forces with some of Malaysia's train devotees, finding a shared and nostalgic affection for the country's older, and less common freight trains.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: Young people were told artificial intelligence would make them sharper, faster, more creative - and more employable. But for many, that promise now feels more complicated. From students leaning on chatbots to write essays to graduates sending out hundreds of AI-assisted job applications - only to be filtered out by algorithms on the other side - artificial intelligence is no longer a future story. Join AI Decoded as we ask: Is AI empowering the next generation - or quietly undermining it?

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: One of the fastest growing sectors in tourism, both in the UK and globally, is being driven by people visiting locations that have been featured in films. In a special edition Talking Movies investigates the booming film tourism business also known as 'set jetting'. In London, Tom Brook joins a James Bond walking tour visiting key locations used in 007 films and talks to people helping to attract visitors through the capital's cinematic history. In Austria, the 1965 classic The Sound of Music brings in an estimated 300,000 visitors each year. Tom joins a group of fans who burst into spontaneous renditions of the musical's beloved songs as they travel through Salzburg's streets. The Oscar-winning picture Anora set in and around Brighton Beach in New York has resulted in a whole new wave of tourists visiting this Russian speaking community. We see some key locations used in the movie and hear from the picture's celebrated director Sean Baker on why he wanted to shoot there. And Tom visits the French town of Dinard which capitalises on movie tourism through its annual film festival in Northern France.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: This week John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, examines the new deal aimed at ending the war between the US and Iran, analyses Ukraine's bid to join the European Union and looks at the worsening situation for women in Afghanistan under Taliban rule.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Giving birth in the UK is getting more dangerous; the number of women dying in childbirth is up 20% in the last ten years. Now, in Nottingham, the largest-ever maternity inquiry is underway, reviewing the medical records of thousands of births for signs of avoidable harm. Reporting for Panorama, Michael Buchanan hears powerful testimonies: from mothers who weren’t listened to, staff who blew the whistle but were ignored, and from parents whose babies’ tragic deaths were dismissed as ‘just bad luck’. The programme also hears from the new chief executive of the Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, who has promised change. As families unite to demand answers, Panorama investigates how systemic neglect, inadequate staffing and a reluctance to learn from mistakes have led to heartbreak.

programme.title: The President's Path
programme.desc: Caitríona Perry is joined by White House reporter Bernd Buzman and CBS News political director Fin Gomez to unpack the memorandum of understanding with Iran, signed with a flourish by President Trump before G7 leaders at Versailles. With a 60-day clock now running toward bigger talks, they ask what America has actually gained - given the Strait of Hormuz was open and petrol cheaper before Operation Epic Fury began. The team digs into the unresolved nuclear question, the daunting task of diluting Iran's enriched uranium on a timeline that took the Obama administration 20 months, and the politics driving it all: JD Vance positioning himself as the face of the deal, midterm pressures and an uneasy MAGA base. They also weigh a renewed push on Ukraine, rumours swirling around Cuba, and a strikingly public rift between Trump and Netanyahu. Could the flashpoint in Lebanon yet unravel the whole Iran deal?

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Julie Lin and Peter McKenna, and by special guest Lenny Henry. Drinks expert Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: When he's not touring the world, comedian Ed Byrne loves nothing more than to escape the bright lights for the splendour of the Wicklow mountains, Ireland's largest national park. Ed and Anna hike the Sally Gap, and he surprises her with a meal on the mountainside. After their feast, they search for sika deer in Glendalough with a local park ranger, learning about deer management. Anna also visits a sheep farming family known for their award-winning halloumi-style cheese.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp heads to Kingston upon Thames for this episode. Two teams battle it out at the Kingston Antiques Centre with experts Charles Hanson and Thomas Forrester on hand to help our teams bag a bargain, but who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction? Natasha also meets a local artist and learns about a photographic technique that is over 150 years old, before trying it out herself.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as three-time World Cup finalists Netherlands stay in Texas for their second Group F game, this time in Houston against Sweden. Netherlands were unbeaten in qualifying, scoring 27 goals in eight matches, with Memphis Depay bagging eight. Sweden earned their finals place by beating Poland in the play-offs after missing out on the World Cup four years ago. Both the previous meetings between these sides at major tournaments finished 0-0, with the only World Cup encounter back in 1974.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Stevie faces the consequences of her actions.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: The group are trapped indoors by a heatwave and Jess determines to get her family on the next boat home. Tensions mount as the power shorts, the stolen money resurfaces and unwanted visitors threaten to destroy Zoe’s chance at happiness with Solomon. Can she escape what the Fates seem to have in store for her? Nat and Jacob have some making up to do but as the temperature soars, they too are pushed to breaking point. This isn’t going to end well.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The gang belatedly get involved in the Great Famine, the one thing they didn’t need to get involved in. Callum’s new job at the Ballymac Folk Museum sends him down a spiral of method acting as a starving peasant. And he’s helped along in this grovelling subservience by his new boss, tyrannical land steward Phillip. The sudden news, to Jordan, that a million people died during the potato famine causes him to finally develop empathy, and it turns out to be a real pain.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Strange things are afoot in Ballymacnoose: animals in small clothing, paranormal sightings of leprechauns, and most bizarrely of all, Callum gets his first ever match on a dating app. The one prickle on the pickle is that Gwen, Callum’s dream girl, is also in an open relationship with the 50-year-old Grainne, a woman who disembowels chickens for a living. Meanwhile, Gemma becomes obsessed with dressing farm animals in tiny clothing and causes Sammy to spin out in paranoia thinking that his farm has become infested with leprechauns. So Lorcan has no other choice but to introduce Gemma to the dark secrets of the Ballymacnoose Paranormal Society, also run by his da and Frank.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Life in Ballymacnoose becomes very complicated when Callum accidentally walks in on Gemma and Lorcan trying out a new form of lovemaking. The sight triggers a long-buried childhood trauma in Callum and raises uncomfortable questions about the town’s deeply repressed relationship with sex. Can Ballymacnoose open its heart to true love? Meanwhile, Jordan has officially begun dating his much older lover, 50-year-old Grainne. But as their relationship deepens, Jordan is confronted with another harsh reality of adulthood: he’s going bald. Faced with the slow retreat of his hairline and the love of a good woman, Jordan must decide whether to cling to denial or embrace manhood in all its forms - love, responsibility and mushroom carbonaras.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: As Callum begins slowly unravelling under the strain of Jordan's relentless night terrors about baldness, he stumbles upon a flyer for Ballymacbot Wars - a local robot combat tournament offering a generous cash prize and potential ticket out of Jordan’s bedroom. Meanwhile, Jordan is uncharacteristically uninterested in the whole affair. He's become a bald hermit, terrified to leave the house and expose his creamy scalp to the outside world. That is, until, like a dagger in the night, a hooded burst of inspiration sends him back out into the world… and the robot wars.

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special cricket edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman, with contestants Alison Mitchell, Andy Zaltzman, Devon Malcolm, Graham Gooch, Georgie Heath, Fran Wilson, Graeme Swann and Mark Ramprakash.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as World Cup debutants Curacao play their second match in Group E against Ecuador at Kansas City Stadium. Curacao are the smallest nation ever to qualify for a World Cup and have the oldest coach in the tournament's history: 78-year-old former Rangers and Sunderland manager Dick Advocaat. Ecuador impressively finished second in South American qualifying and boast Chelsea midfielder Moises Caicedo in their ranks. It’s 20 years since their best performance at a World Cup, making the last 16 in 2006, when they were knocked out by England.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Tunisia play their second game in Group F against Japan at Monterrey Stadium. Tunisia’s shock group-stage win against France in Qatar in 2022 was only their third victory across six World Cup appearances. This is their third successive appearance at the tournament. Japan made their World Cup debut in 1998 and alternated between getting knocked out in the group stage and reaching the round of 16 in their first six appearances. They broke the cycle four years ago by making the knockout stage for the second time in a row. Brighton’s Kaoru Mitoma and Daizen Maeda of Celtic could play key roles.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: The big names behind the big stories. Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: A service from the Shree Swaminaryan Temple in Cardiff. The Hindu Aarti service, presented in Gujarati, is a ceremony of light, offering thanks and devotion to the deities.

programme.title: Early Man
programme.desc: Young Dug’s sheltered life with his Stone Age tribe is shattered by bronze-mining invaders led by greedy Lord Nooth. To win his home back, desperate Dug gambles on challenging these more sophisticated folk in a contest they love, but he only knows from rock paintings: football. Animated comedy fun from Aardman.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher explores how faith has shaped the stories of inspiring father figures, past and present, and celebrates the image of God as a loving father through favourite hymns.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television programmes. Whether you’ve seen something you’ve loved or loathed on the BBC, get in touch with your point of view. Email: pov@bbc.co.uk X: @bbcPoV Facebook: Search for BBC Points of View

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Spain play their second game in Group H against Saudi Arabia at the Atlanta Stadium. As the reigning European champions, Spain will be favourites to win Group H, but they struggled at the last World Cup, losing in the group stages against Japan and being knocked out at the Round of 16 by Morocco. Saudi Arabia secured one of the greatest ever World Cup giant-killings four years ago when they beat eventual champions Argentina. The only time they have reached the knockout stages came when the tournament was last held in the US in 1994.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Away from Hampshire's famous ports and bustling harbours lies a wild world of woodland, heathland and chalk streams. Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith meet the people working to find, protect and celebrate the county's lesser-spotted wildlife. Charlotte also investigates how proposed changes to visa rules could affect sheep shearing in the UK.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow travels to beautiful Beaumaris Castle on the island of Anglesey, north Wales, where presenter Fiona Bruce learns about the history and heritage of the Menai Bridge. Treasures discovered by our team of experts include a dog collar from 1765, a Royal Doulton vase with a dragon decoration and an atlas of Anglesey. Joanna Hardy is thrilled to find jewellery once owned by Lady Megan Lloyd George, daughter of David Lloyd George, the former prime minister. She was the first female MP for Wales and represented Anglesey. Lisa Lloyd spots an articulated Saba doll made in the 1920s, and watch-and-clock specialist Ben Wright values a large pocket watch, also known as a coach watch. Heading north from Anglesey lies the Isle of Man, where Marc Allum finds a collection of trophies from the Isle of Man Tourist Trophy (TT) road racing event for motorcycles. And Fiona’s observational skills are put to the test by Asian art specialist Alexandra Aguilar, who presents three objects that may not be all they first seem.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: A sudden death at the funeral of a rugby club groundsman leads John and Janie to suspect everyone in the club. With Janie’s big move looming, John considers life without her.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as World Cup debutants Cape Verde play their second match in Group H, taking on former winners Uruguay in Miami. Cape Verde, a group of islands in the Atlantic Ocean with a population of just over 500,000, finished top of their qualifying group, beating Cameroon along the way. The Blue Sharks have twice reached the quarter-finals of the Africa Cup of Nations. Uruguay, winners of the inaugural tournament in 1930, are managed by former Leeds manager Marcelo Bielsa and qualified for a fourth consecutive World Cup, beating Brazil and Argentina in the process. They are captained by Real Madrid’s Federico Valverde.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Documentary series following the dedicated fraud fighters pursuing the criminals who steal billions of pounds a year from the public purse. In this episode, the Environment Agency discovers toxic waste being dumped illegally in a quarry south of Bristol. There is a risk that the waste will contaminate the local water supply, meaning a potential £15 billion bill for taxpayers. The alarm is raised when an inspector visits the landfill site and finds huge amounts of waste that the site is not permitted to handle. After a detailed investigation, they discover that the owner of the site is taking in large amounts of potentially hazardous waste from all over the country. He doesn’t have the right permits and is not paying any of the landfill tax that should be due on this type of waste. It’s a massive hit to the public purse. In our second case, a hospital surgeon and prolific TikToker forges his own payslips to defraud the NHS out of £28,000. He’d been working for years as a locum in a hospital and was claiming from his agency for many more hours than he’d actually worked. He used the money to fund a lavish lifestyle – something he was happy to flaunt to his followers on TikTok.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Since the time of the Saxons, a horn has been blown in Ripon’s market square every single night. It’s believed to be the oldest continuous ceremony in the world! Danny Sebastian meets four local men and women whose job it is to sound the horn, come rain or shine, in a custom dating back more than a thousand years. But which team will be blowing their own trumpet at the auction at the end of the programme? On the red team are a musical mum and daughter Louise and Sue. In blue are teaching friends Inderjit and Emma, who are not only work colleagues but watch the show together! Their companions for the shopping at Wetherby Racecourse are experts Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson. Auctioneer Caroline Hawley is on the rostrum to sell their lots - gavel in hand.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A haul of suspected cannabis plants is found at a residential home, stolen bike parts are discovered in a derelict hotel, and dogs sniff out drugs at the airport.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: In a marathon chase lasting several hours, Rav and Nick are locked in a struggle to track down an elderly woman who is being swindled by scammers pretending to fix her computer. They know she’s in a care home, but pinning down which one proves extremely difficult, leading to a team-wide effort as the scam hub squad call countless potential addresses. With the vulnerable woman edging ever closer to paying out, the team are forced to pull out all the stops to prevent an international transfer being made directly from her online banking. Can they track her down in time to put a permanent stop to the scammers’ criminal efforts?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having fallen in love with Norfolk a little more with each visit, a couple have decided to make the county their forever home, and Denise Nurse is there to help them find their forever house. Denise also gets the opportunity to take the helm of a newly restored boat which took part in Operation Dynamo, the 1940 evacuation of British troops from Dunkirk.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Lee Burkhill are in Salford to transform Lottie and Mead's backyard, which is a bit of an eyesore, into something cool and edgy that epitomises their urban lifestyle and that will capture the heart of their Edwardian home. Flo joins Charlie Dimmock in Southampton to turn Claire and daughter Norah's small garden and World War II bunker into a magical space where mum and daughter can spend quality time together. The challenge facing them is that half the garden is taken up by a massive bomb shelter. Claire would love to be able to use the shelter as a space for Norah to enjoy, turning it into a play space full of fairy magic.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as defending champions Argentina play their second Group J match against Austria in Dallas. With 38-year-old Lionel Messi playing in his sixth World Cup, Argentina will look to go all the way again four years on from their triumph in Qatar. The holders played in Dallas during the group stage of the 1994 edition when they were beaten 2-0 by Bulgaria. Austria, managed by former Manchester United boss Ralf Rangnick, are making their first appearance at a World Cup since 1998. They impressed at Euro 2024 finishing top of their group ahead of France and the Netherlands.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea drives a wedge between Honey and Billy, the Fox-Truemans receive upsetting news, and Suki struggles with Vinny.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of 2022 runners-up France’s second game in Group I against Iraq in Philadelphia, the first meeting between the two countries. After reaching the last two finals, France remain among the favourites to win the tournament, which would be a sweet parting gift for manager Didier Deschamps, who will leave Les Bleus after 14 years in charge. Iraq are led by Australian Graham Arnold, whose coaching team includes former Manchester United boss Rene Meulensteen, and are aiming to get out of the group, having failed to do so at their only previous World Cup in 1986.

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa
programme.desc: To save his own job, Alan gets radio colleague Pat laid off, but when the man returns to the relaunched station with a shotgun and a siege ensues, Alan is given the high-profile role of go-between, trying to appease both the hostage taker and armed police. So lots of people have Alan in their sights. Hit big screen spin-off for the self-obsessed former TV 'personality', from BBC Films.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Facing a dilemma, Siobhan makes a plan with Linda to meet the lads on a bus. An unexpected intervention by local bully Billy Murphy leads to an accidental wild bus ride around Cork, ultimately bringing everyone closer together.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. His girlfriend moves out when a rat appears in his flat, then menacing rival dealer Psycho Paul has a score to settle.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. While his girlfriend is away, Moz tries to seduce the beautiful China, but his plans are interrupted by another appearance from the rat.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz learns about a local murder, meets his stalker and makes up with Nicki. Meanwhile, China's new boyfriend Cartoon Head has something to hide.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz gives a local band a hand and the police help him shake off his stalker. After China splits with Cartoon Head, Moz is keen to provide a shoulder to cry on.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: It’s the first day of sixth form, and Mike is looking forward to a bit of social mobility. He thinks he’s way better than the group of so-called 'loser' friends he’s somehow accrued, and aspires to higher things. But for now, he’s stuck with powergeek Sonny and Dan, a lad who only communicates in put-downs, and he’s going to find it hard to shake them off: Mike, Sonny and Dan all take the mobility bus to school. Mike has cerebral palsy, Sonny uses a wheelchair, and Dan ‘Syndrome’ Simmons has Down’s Syndrome. So Mike, Sonny and Dan are trapped together – at least for the ride to school.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara face their last Christmas. Trapped on an Arctic base, under attack from terrifying creatures, who are you going to call? Santa Claus!

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda has decided to prove to herself and her friends that she can officially be an adult. She starts by looking after a toddler, but the soft play centre is too much of a lure and she gets stuck in a kids' tunnel. She then hosts a dinner party for eight, which ends in inevitable chaos.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda has been caring for her ill mother for five very long days. Trapped in her flat and being barked at by Penny, she is forced to think about her life regrets. Before she has the chance to tick off her bucket list she gets ill, and Tilly, Gary and Stevie offer no sympathy or help at all.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The Rose family's lives are changed forever when the government comes to collect years of back taxes. Left with almost nothing, they must move to Johnny’s last remaining asset, Schitt’s Creek. When Johnny offends the town’s mayor, Roland Schitt, the Rose family soon discovers that the doors to their motel rooms have been taken. It’s now up to Johnny to set things right in the hope that his family can sleep safely on their first night in their new home.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: After Johnny awakes soaking wet from a leaking pipe in the ceiling, he decides that he must sell the town, but soon discovers that this is easier said than done. He needs to get Roland’s approval before he can put the town on the market. Meanwhile, the Rose children check out the local nightlife by attending a tailgate party. Alexis uses the opportunity to scope for a new boyfriend, while David gets to know Stevie, the girl that runs the motel.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny is shocked to see the pornographic-looking town sign and plans to get it taken down. When he talks to the town council about it, he finds out that the sign is very important to Roland for family reasons. Meanwhile, David is faced with the near-impossible task of finding a job, while Alexis starts her community service and is paired with Mutt, the guy she kissed the night before.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira come to a shocking realisation that they might be terrible parents. They attempt to get to know their twenty-something children, but David and Alexis avoid their parents' intrusive interest, with David trying to sell some of his clothes for extra cash and Alexis investigating Mutt’s mysterious relationship with the mayor’s wife. Johnny and Moira speak with Roland and Jocelyn about parenting and realise that all parents have issues with their children.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown has family troubles to contend with: her son Dermot has fallen out with Maria Nicholson, his long-term girlfriend, and Grandad is feeling poorly. After a sleepless night, Mrs Brown decides to try to sort out Dermot and Maria's problem by giving Maria the benefit of her advice. But her heart-to-heart with Maria only succeeds in making the dispute worse. So Mrs Brown's daughter Cathy intervenes. She's a psychology student, and offers Dermot and Maria a counselling session. A dismayed Mrs Brown can't help interfering, and the session quickly descends into a blazing row between her and Cathy... with an effect on Dermot and Maria that not even Agnes Brown could have predicted. Meanwhile, Grandad ends up in hospital when Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes dramatically wrong. And Cathy reveals the electrifying secret of how she keeps dodgy men at bay.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown is concerned that her family are keeping too many secrets. Her daughter Cathy won't let her meet her new boyfriend. Her elder son Mark won't reveal why his feisty wife Betty has thrown him out. And her younger son Dermot has lied to his fiancee Maria Nicholson's well-to-do parents about the status of his job. When Betty and Mrs Brown meet to discuss Mark's problem, they unsurprisingly come to blows. Unfortunately their row happens in front of Dermot's posh future mother-in-law Mrs Nicholson, who has come round to discuss arrangements for her daughter's wedding. Horrified by what she witnesses, Mrs Nicholson decides that the Brown family are so rough she will try to stop them attending the wedding. Then Mrs Brown spots Betty with another man, and concludes that Betty and Mark have fallen out because Betty is having an affair. But when she finally confronts Betty, Agnes is confounded to discover that Mark is the one who has something to hide: a guilty secret for which Agnes has only herself to blame.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is keen to improve her mind but realises that neither poetry nor biscuits are the answer. Gaz is desperate to feel attractive and thinks that the best way to accomplish this is to flash his rather underdeveloped chest.

programme.title: Funboys

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: It’s Sienna’s first day at the PRU and a chance for a fresh start. She makes a new friend in troubled Belle, reunites with frosty old schoolmate Jaeden and crosses swords with hyperactive Halil, who is refusing to take his ADHD medication. When Halil’s behaviour threatens the class paintballing trip, Sienna takes matters into her own hands, with disastrous consequences.

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: The kids are introduced to the ‘power of manifestation’ by holier-than-thou life coach Noah. However, Sienna takes a dislike to him and leads a rebellion. Meanwhile, Belle contemplates sending a nude, Jaeden struggles with home responsibilities, and Halil is left shocked as his manifestations appear to come true.

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: Romance is in the air as Jean and Anthony take the kids camping in the woods. The kids struggle to navigate the area, the elements and, ultimately, each other. They have to plant the flag before sundown, but an emergency arises when Belle goes missing. Meanwhile, the teachers get carried away and have a few too many, while Jaeden’s arachnophobia threatens to get the better of him.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
